front: Film: a house facade falls around Keaton's character during a cyclone; he survives because of a window opening; shoes nailed to the ground so he wouldn't move
Film: just before the house stunt, Keaton's character wakes up in a hospital to find his bed exposed to the elements by a windstorm
Film: Dana Stevens's 2022 biography Cameraman connects the falling-house stunt metaphorically to the hurricane going through Keaton's career
Film: Steve McQueen's 1997 film Deadpan re-creates the falling-house stunt; the title references Keaton's stone-faced persona
Film: Johnnie Gray pursues Union spies who stole his locomotive (The General) in the locomotive The Texas; ends with Johnnie embracing Annabelle with his left hand while saluting with his right
Film: Johnnie smuggles his girlfriend Annabelle Lee in a burlap sack full of shoes; a bridge collapses under the weight of The Texas
Film: Keaton plays a film projectionist; a villain 'the local sheik' blames the theft of a pocket watch on him; in a dream sequence, the protagonist enters the movie he's projecting
Film: uses trick pool shots to avoid hitting an explosive number 13 ball, having already replaced it
Director: known as the 'Great Stone Face'; debut was in The Butcher Boy alongside Fatty Arbuckle
Director: Gilles Deleuze argued his use of 'trajectories' and 'machines' creates a unique link between a small hero and a giant situation
Director: coached Lucille Ball in a scene for an unaired TV pilot that was instrumental in the creation of I Love Lucy
